# Hiking Summary — 2026-06-18

**Generated by:** hike-izer Skill (CARD-0073)
**Data source:** JCTsh Environmental Data Google Sheets (Environmental Data, Hiking Observations, GPS Track), via `action=export`, filtered to `source=hiking-monitor`
**Note:** the prior evening's hike (June 17) ran past midnight into the early hours of this day but is attributed to June 17, the day it started — see `2026-06-17_hike-summary.md`. This is a separate, shorter, later-morning hike.

---

## The Hike

A shorter, brighter outing than the night before — this one ran from mid-morning into midday, with the sun climbing steadily higher and swinging from due east toward more southeasterly as the hike went on, never dropping toward the horizon the way the previous evening's hike had. Conditions were noticeably milder and drier than the day before, and elevation gain over the route was a bit more concentrated given the shorter duration.

The device logged only sparsely for the rest of the day outside this hike — coverage today was thin. Battery voltage was also visibly lower than the previous day's readings, continuing a downward trend rather than holding steady — worth watching, given this trip's already-documented LiPo failure later on.

No voice observations were recorded on this hike.

## Expected vs. Actual Data Coverage — Pipeline Health Check

Environmental Data: 133 hiking-monitor readings against 720 expected for a full day (18.5% coverage) — considerably lower than June 17's, meaning the device spent most of this day logging little or nothing outside the hike itself. GPS correlation matched 82 of the 133 readings (61.7%) — noticeably weaker than the previous day, though still consistent with the same ±5-minute correlation-window geometry already documented, not a new problem.

Same UV caveat as June 17 (CARD-0065) applies here.

**Battery trend worth flagging directly:** 3.00V–4.00V today versus 3.97V–4.36V the day before — a real, continuing decline day-over-day, not just noise. This is consistent with the field LiPo failure already documented for this trip (`README.md`, 2026-07-03) — this day's data is likely an early data point in that decline, not an isolated reading.
